title: I Waited So Long for THIS All-In-One Price Action Indicator!
author: SoheilPKO
summary_bullets: I want to show something amazing, a new indicator that gives you a dashboard related to market structure and price action concepts.
It is an all-in-one indicator combining order block, fair value gap, liquidity sweep, volume analysis, volatility analysis, swing high and swing low, market structure labels, and many more concepts.
And the excellent feature is that all these concepts can be displayed independently in up to seven different timeframes and everything is shown here in this dashboard.
This is the best indicator I've ever seen on TradingView.
I will completely explain this indicator and how you can use it.
All right, on TradingView platform, here we click on indicators and we search market structure dashboard, this one, which is written by flux chart.
All right, as you can see, a dashboard is added to our chart here.
There are lots of information here, but don't worry, I will completely explain everything.
This dashboard provides some information related to market structure and price action concepts, and it has the ability to show them independently for different timeframes.
By default, no level or information is shown on the chart, and everything is only here in the dashboard.

creator_notes.quote: All right, let's begin from here.
creator_notes.note: Meaning: All right, let's begin from here.

extracted_rules: If we go to the settings of the indicator here, you can see that we have settings related to timeframes.

You can have up to seven different timeframes and you can set if, for example, you use three timeframes in your trading strategy, you can remove the other ones and adjust timeframes based on your trading strategy.
And in case if you want to change them, you need to consider this fact that the higher the timeframe, the more reliable the trends.
If, for example, we go to one minute timeframe, you can see that we have information for seven timeframes that are set in the settings.
If we go to the settings of the indicator, and if we scroll down here, we have settings for visual overlays.
When you hover on each of these information, the indicator gives a small explanation.
If we go to weekly timeframe, we can see that yes, the price swept the liquidity and turned back down.
If we go to the settings of the indicator and if we scroll down here, we can turn on order blocks.

In case if it is your first time hearing about order blocks, order block is a price range that institutional traders, banks, hedge funds, investment companies or other large financial entities, they entered or exited the market with very large capital.
If I want to say it in a very simple word, order blocks are price ranges where relatively large amounts of orders are executed.
So we can consider them in our trading strategy for trade entry, stop loss and profit target.
If we change the time frame to weekly time frame, we can clearly see that the price is inside the identified bearish order block.
If we go to the settings of the indicator, and if we scroll down here, we have an option for fair value gaps, we can turn it on.
By default, the EMA length is set at nine, but you can change it in the settings if you need higher length, for example, in your trading strategy.
In the settings of the indicator, there are some other concepts or features that you can turn them on if you need those features and concepts in your trading strategy.
If you have any questions, feel free to ask and leave a comment for me.

strategy_components.description: In front of each timeframe, there is also weight of the timeframe for trend calculation.
And in case if you want to change them, you need to consider this fact that the higher the timeframe, the more reliable the trends.
So they should have higher weight in trend calculation.
So depending on your trading strategy and the timeframes that you need, you can have your required timeframes here.
So we can consider them in our trading strategy for trade entry, stop loss and profit target.
And here we have trend bias, which gives us information about the major trend based on higher time frame analysis.
This indicator by providing a multi time frame dashboard, it can help you to effectively analyze the market and have your required information all in one in a single dashboard on your price chart.

explicit_conditions: So we can consider them in our trading strategy for trade entry, stop loss and profit target.
